rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by resolving or removing invalid expressions before applying repairs.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign only for output features or declared auxiliary variables.
generic_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report '<error>' types, trace and resolve the first invalid identifier or helper declaration on the same line.
Ensure all AGREE expressions reference only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers.
Validate that component type contracts do not reference implementation-specific subcomponent ports unless properly declared.
Use assign statements in implementation scopes only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must resolve to valid types; avoid '<error>' by ensuring all identifiers are properly declared or referenced.
In AGREE contracts, left and right sides of binary expressions must evaluate to boolean types.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE type errors; instead, fix or remove invalid clauses.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output features or auxiliary variables.
